main.description: A guestbook — the 'book' itself, living on the host's PDS. Visitors sign it by writing an is.dame.guestbook.entry record on their OWN repo whose `subject` points at this record's at-uri; the backlinks (indexed by services like Constellation) ARE the guestbook's pages. Singleton for now (rkey 'self'), but nothing prevents a host from keeping several books under other keys someday.

title.description: Display title of the book, e.g. 'the dame.is guestbook'.

description.description: What signing this book means, shown to would-be signers.

url.description: Where the guestbook renders, e.g. https://dame.is/guestbook.

hidden.description: Entry at-uris the host has tucked out of public display. Moderation-by-curation: the host can't touch a signer's record (only the signer can delete it), so the book instead records which signatures it declines to show. Kept on the book so moderation state is itself a portable public record any renderer can honor.
